prompt: TouchDesigner is RUNNING and the live tools are connected. My workflow edits shader FILES on disk, and I need proof the toolchain catches a bad edit: TD auto-reloads a file-synced DAT but the GLSL op only recompiles on its next cook, so a naive check reads stale status. There is a working pixel shader at {{RUN_DIR}}/assets/eval_fragment.glsl. Work entirely inside a fresh container you create at /eval_s17_scratch (touch nothing else in the project, and do NOT save the project at any point). Look up the GLSL TOP and Text DAT file-sync parameters in the knowledge base first. Then: (1) inside the scratch container, create a Text DAT synced to that shader file (file parameter + sync-to-file on) and a GLSL TOP using that DAT as its pixel shader; (2) confirm it compiles by calling the GLSL status tool with the file_path argument — the shader FILE path, not a node path; (3) break the shader by rewriting the file ON DISK (you can write the file from a Python script), then call the GLSL status tool with file_path again and quote me exactly what it reports; (4) restore the original working shader to the file, confirm the status is clean again via file_path, and delete the whole /eval_s17_scratch container. Report: the file_path status output for the broken state (quoted), the clean recheck, and confirmation of cleanup.
notes: W-A2 regression scenario (PR #26 commits 3d9ac78/9f4d5a3): get_glsl_status(file_path) resolves the synced DAT(s) by file par (exact normalized path, then basename fallback), finds the GLSL op(s) they feed, FORCE-COOKS them (the recompile-only-on-next-cook trap), and reports per-op status headed '— shader file <path>'. The 'shader file' tool_result_re pins the file_path code path specifically; the FAILED/OK pair pins detection through a real on-disk edit. Fixture eval_fragment.glsl is generated by fixtures/make_fixtures.py (self-hosted, nothing binary committed). Born aspirational; td_live_running gated. v2 (2026-07-16): cleanup asserted as an OUTCOME via live.absent, replacing v1's `tool_called: delete_td_node` (see s15 notes for the mechanism-over-outcome defect). KNOWN WEAK, deliberately left alone in v2: the `tool_called: execute_python_script` assertion is a near-vacuous proxy for 'the agent rewrote the file ON DISK' — that tool is also called for unrelated work, and exec_node_method + a DAT .save() would satisfy the on-disk edit without it. The FAILED->OK file_path pair is the real evidence and is strictly stronger, though it too could in principle be produced by editing the DAT text rather than the file. Retiring the proxy needs an on-disk outcome assertion the harness does not have (the file is restored by design, so its final state cannot witness the intermediate edit) — owner call, out of scope for this fix.
